title: Zero-config quickstart: 5 lines from npm install to verified handshake

artifacts.description: BolyraClient class exposing new BolyraClient({ provider }) and client.handshake(humanSecret, agentCredential) — orchestrates artifact resolution, Merkle fetch, nonce gen, prove, and verify in one call
ArtifactResolver: resolves WASM and zkey paths relative to the installed npm package directory (require.resolve('@bolyra/sdk/circuits/...')), with fallback to BOLYRA_ARTIFACTS_DIR env var
MerkleProofFetcher: queries the on-chain HumanRegistry contract via the caller-supplied ethers/viem provider to fetch the current Merkle root and sibling path for a given identity commitment
generateSessionNonce(): crypto.randomBytes(32) wrapped as a typed SessionNonce — single-use, not replayable
Re-export BolyraClient alongside existing public API symbols; bump minor version to 0.3.0
Integration test: stubs ArtifactResolver and MerkleProofFetcher, exercises handshake() end-to-end with mock proofs; separate slow-path test with FULL_PROOF=1 against compiled artifacts
Unit test: verifies ArtifactResolver finds the correct WASM/zkey paths in the local build, and that a missing artifact throws ArtifactNotFoundError with an actionable message
Rewrite opening section to show the 5-line pattern; retain advanced section documenting the low-level proveHandshake/verifyHandshake API for callers who need custom path control

steps: Add circuit WASM and zkey files as package-included assets in sdk/package.json `files` array (or symlink from circuits/build/ via postinstall script) so require.resolve can locate them after npm install

Implement ArtifactResolver (sdk/src/artifacts.ts): check BOLYRA_ARTIFACTS_DIR env first, then resolve via require.resolve('@bolyra/sdk') dirname traversal, throw ArtifactNotFoundError with npm install hint on miss
Implement generateSessionNonce (sdk/src/nonce.ts): crypto.randomBytes(32), return as branded SessionNonce type so callers cannot pass an arbitrary Buffer

Implement MerkleProofFetcher (sdk/src/merkle.ts): accept an ethers Provider or viem PublicClient (union type), call HumanRegistry.getMerkleProof(identityCommitment), return typed MerkleProof; cache root per block to avoid redundant RPC calls

Implement BolyraClient class (sdk/src/client.ts): constructor accepts { provider, artifactsDir? }; handshake(humanSecret, agentCredential) method: (a) derive identity commitment from humanSecret, (b) call MerkleProofFetcher, (c) call generateSessionNonce, (d) call existing proveHandshake with resolved paths, (e) call existing verifyHandshake, (f) return { verified: boolean, nullifierHash, sessionNonce }
Export BolyraClient and companion types (ArtifactNotFoundError, SessionNonce, HandshakeResult) from sdk/src/index.ts; keep existing exports untouched
Write unit tests (sdk/test/artifacts.test.ts) for ArtifactResolver path logic with real filesystem fixtures
Write integration tests (sdk/test/client.test.ts): mock-proof fast path using sinon stubs on ArtifactResolver and MerkleProofFetcher; guard slow real-proof path behind FULL_PROOF=1
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md with the 5-line example, provider setup note (ethers.js or viem), and link to advanced API docs
Bump sdk/package.json version to 0.3.0, run tsc --noEmit, npm run build, confirm artifact paths resolve in the built dist/
dependencies: circuits/build/HumanUniqueness.wasm and HumanUniqueness_final.zkey must exist (npm run compile:circuits)
contracts/HumanRegistry deployed address and ABI available in sdk/src/contracts/ (already generated by Hardhat typechain)
ethers v6 or viem already peer-dep of SDK (confirm in sdk/package.json before introducing union provider type)
Existing proveHandshake and verifyHandshake low-level API in sdk/src/index.ts (no changes to those signatures)
Node 18+ crypto.randomBytes available (no additional dep needed for nonce)
